name: 45 Degree Hyperextension
shortDescription: A lower back exercise on a 45-degree hyperextension bench that targets the spinal erectors and posterior chain.
instructions: Set up on a 45-degree hyperextension bench with hips supported and ankles secured. Cross arms over the chest or place hands behind the head. Hinge at the hips, lower your torso toward the floor, then contract lower back and glutes to return to start.
steps: Position yourself on the 45-degree hyperextension bench with ankles secured.
Cross arms over chest or place hands behind head.
Lower your upper body forward at the hips.
Stop when your upper body is just below parallel to the floor.
Lift your torso back up in line with your legs.
breathing: Exhale as you rise, inhale as you lower.
